zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Dinan於大約1062年出生在英國英格蘭戴文努特維爾時，他的父親Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan25歲，母親Ganna Belleme28歲。他於1087年與Radegonde Nerondes在法國布列塔尼迪南結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1128年在英國薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey de Dinan于大约1062年出生在英国英格兰戴文努特維爾时，他的父亲Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan25岁，母亲Ganna Belleme28岁。他于1087年与Radegonde Nerondes在法国布列塔尼迪南结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1128年在英国薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dinan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (now mainly counties Clare and Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Daghnáin ‘descendant of Daghnán’, possibly a diminutive of dagh ‘good’.
French: habitational name from Dinan, in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany. This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Dineen
meanings: Irish (Cork and Limerick): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinnín ‘descendant of Duinnín’, a byname from a diminutive of donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Dinan was born about 1062, in Nutwell, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan, was 25 and his mother, Ganna Belleme, was 28. He married Radegonde Nerondes in 1087, in Dinan, Brittany, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1128, in West Buckland, Somerset, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Dinan est né environ 1062, à Nutwell, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Ganna Belleme, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Radegonde Nerondes en 1087, à Dinan, Bretagne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1128, à West Buckland, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Dinan um 1062 in Nutwell, Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan 25 und seine Mutter Ganna Belleme 28. Er hat 1087 in Dinan, Bretagne, Frankreich Radegonde Nerondes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1128 im Alter von 67 Jahren in West Buckland, Somerset, England und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Dinan è nato il circa 1062 nel Nutwell, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Ganna Belleme, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Radegonde Nerondes nel 1087, nel Dinan, Bretagne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1128, nel West Buckland, Somerset, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Dinanが約1062年にイギリスイギリスデボンヌトウエルで生まれた時，父Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinanは25歳，母Ganna Bellemeは28歳であった。1087年にフランスブレタグンディナンでRadegonde Nerondesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1128年にイギリスソメルセトウエスト ブックランドで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Dinan(이)가 대략 1062년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 누트웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Ganna Belleme(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1087년 프랑스 브레타그니 딘아에서 Radegonde Nerondes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1128년 영국 서머세트 웨스트 벅랜드에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Dinan nasceu aproximadamente 1062, em Nutwell, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Ganna Belleme, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Radegonde Nerondes em 1087, em Dinan, Bretagne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1128, em West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Dinan родился около 1062 г. в местности нутвелл, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Ganna Belleme, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Radegonde Nerondes в 1087 г. в местности динан, бретагн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1128 г. в местности вест буккланд, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey de Dinan nació aproximadamente 1062, en Nutwell, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Oliver I de Dinan, Vicomte de Dinan, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Ganna Belleme, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Radegonde Nerondes en 1087, en Dinan, Bretagne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1128, en West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
